---
title: "PyApprox UQ Tutorial Library"
subtitle: "Self-contained tutorials for uncertainty quantification"
---

## Overview

This library contains modular tutorials covering uncertainty quantification (UQ) methods implemented in the `pyapprox.typing` module. Each tutorial is self-contained and can be completed independently, though they build on each other in suggested learning paths.

**Primary Example:** Tutorials use the Lotka-Volterra 3-species ODE system as a running example, demonstrating UQ methods on a realistic ecological model.

## Tutorial Catalog

### Getting Started

| Tutorial | Description | Prerequisites |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| [Setup Environment](setup_environment.qmd) | Configure Python environment for PyApprox | None |
| [UQ Problem Framing](uq_problem_framing.qmd) | Core concepts of uncertainty quantification | Setup |

### Forward UQ

| Tutorial | Description | Prerequisites |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| [Forward Model Definition](forward_model_definition.qmd) | Define and evaluate forward models | Setup |
| [ODE Integration Infrastructure](ode_integration.qmd) | ODE solvers and time stepping methods | Forward Model |
| [Random Variables & Distributions](random_variables_distributions.qmd) | Probability distributions for uncertain inputs | Setup |
| [Quantities of Interest](quantities_of_interest.qmd) | Extract meaningful outputs from models | Forward Model |
| [Monte Carlo Basics](monte_carlo_basics.qmd) | Sampling-based uncertainty propagation | Distributions |

### Sensitivity Analysis

| Tutorial | Description | Prerequisites |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| [Sobol Sensitivity Analysis](sobol_sensitivity_analysis.qmd) | Variance-based sensitivity indices | MC Basics |

### Inverse Problems

| Tutorial | Description | Prerequisites |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| [Bayesian Calibration Intro](bayesian_calibration_intro.qmd) | Parameter inference from data | MC Basics |
| [Hamiltonian MCMC](hamiltonian_mcmc.qmd) | Advanced MCMC sampling | Bayesian Calibration |

### Surrogates

| Tutorial | Description | Prerequisites |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| [Polynomial Chaos Expansion](polynomial_chaos_expansion.qmd) | Spectral surrogate models | MC Basics |
| [Introduction to Sparse Grids](sparse_grid_intro.qmd) | Smolyak combination technique | PCE |
| [Adaptive Sparse Grids](adaptive_sparse_grid.qmd) | Goal-oriented sparse grid refinement | Sparse Grids |
| [Gaussian Process Surrogates](gaussian_process_surrogates.qmd) | Kriging/GP regression | MC Basics |

### Multifidelity Methods

| Tutorial | Description | Prerequisites |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| [Control Variates](control_variates.qmd) | Variance reduction fundamentals | MC Basics |
| [Multifidelity Monte Carlo](multifidelity_monte_carlo.qmd) | Combining models of varying fidelity | Control Variates |

### Advanced Topics

| Tutorial | Description | Prerequisites |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| [Optimal Experimental Design](optimal_experimental_design.qmd) | Information-theoretic design | GP Surrogates |
| [Optimization Under Uncertainty](optimization_under_uncertainty.qmd) | Risk-aware optimization | MC Basics |
| [Backend Alternatives](backend_alternatives.qmd) | Using PyTorch backend | Setup |
| [Benchmark Setup](benchmark_setup.qmd) | Creating custom UQ benchmarks | Forward Model |

## Learning Paths

### Path 1: UQ Fundamentals (4 hours)

Recommended for newcomers to uncertainty quantification:

1. Setup Environment
2. UQ Problem Framing
3. Forward Model Definition
4. Random Variables & Distributions
5. Quantities of Interest
6. Monte Carlo Basics
7. Sobol Sensitivity Analysis
8. Bayesian Calibration Intro

### Path 2: Surrogate Modeling (3 hours)

For users interested in building surrogate models:

1. Monte Carlo Basics (prerequisite)
2. Polynomial Chaos Expansion
3. Introduction to Sparse Grids
4. Adaptive Sparse Grids
5. Gaussian Process Surrogates

### Path 3: Multifidelity Methods (2 hours)

For users working with multi-resolution models:

1. Monte Carlo Basics (prerequisite)
2. Control Variates
3. Multifidelity Monte Carlo

## Tags Index

### By Topic

- **setup**: Setup Environment
- **concepts**: UQ Problem Framing
- **forward-uq**: Forward Model Definition, ODE Integration, QoI, Monte Carlo Basics
- **probability**: Random Variables & Distributions
- **sampling**: Monte Carlo Basics, Hamiltonian MCMC
- **sensitivity**: Sobol Sensitivity Analysis
- **inference**: Bayesian Calibration, Hamiltonian MCMC
- **surrogate**: PCE, Sparse Grids, Adaptive Sparse Grids, Gaussian Process
- **multifidelity**: Control Variates, MFMC
- **design**: Optimal Experimental Design
- **optimization**: Optimization Under Uncertainty
- **advanced**: Backend Alternatives, Benchmark Setup

### By Difficulty

- **Beginner**: Setup, UQ Problem Framing, Forward Model
- **Intermediate**: Monte Carlo, Sobol SA, Bayesian Calibration
- **Advanced**: HMC, Multifidelity, OED, OUU

## Conventions

All tutorials follow consistent notation and coding standards:

- **Mathematical notation**: See [CONVENTIONS.md](../CONVENTIONS.md)
- **Array shapes**: Samples as columns `(nvars, nsamples)`
- **Backend**: NumPy by default; PyTorch covered in Backend Alternatives

## Getting Help

- [PyApprox Documentation](https://pyapprox.readthedocs.io)
- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/sandialabs/pyapprox/issues)
- [Discussion Forum](https://github.com/sandialabs/pyapprox/discussions)
